title: Smoky Roasted Turkish Walnut Salad
description: A quick, nutty Turkish salad with smoky roasted walnuts, olives, and fresh veggies, perfect for a light lunch.
instructions: In a small bowl, whisk together the olive oil, smoked paprika, salt, black pepper, and dried oregano.
Toss the chopped walnuts in the oil mixture and spread them on a baking sheet in a single layer.
Roast the walnuts for 8–10 minutes, stirring once, until they are lightly browned and fragrant. Remove from the oven and let them cool.
In a large salad bowl, combine the sliced olives, halved cherry tomatoes, chopped parsley, and thinly sliced garlic.
Add the cooled walnuts to the bowl and drizzle with 1 tablespoon of fresh lemon juice.
Drizzle the salad with a drizzle of olive oil and gently toss to combine all the flavors.
Sprinkle the crumbled feta cheese over the top and give the salad a final gentle toss.
Taste and adjust seasoning with additional salt or pepper if desired.
Serve immediately, or chill for up to 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
